task: Turn brightness to the max value.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The Settings app is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings | Step 3: Adjust the brightness slider to maximum

[Decision]
The goal is to turn the brightness to the maximum value, which requires accessing the system settings. Since the Settings app is not directly visible on this home screen page, I need to open the Settings app first. I will use a system-level open command to launch it directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the Settings app main menu. Visible options include Network & internet, Connected devices, Apps, Notifications, Battery (100%), Storage, and Sound & vibration. The "Display" option is not currently visible on the screen.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings → | Step 3: Adjust the brightness slider to maximum

[Decision]
The current step in the plan is to navigate to Display settings. Since the "Display" option is not in the currently visible list of settings items (which ends at Sound & vibration), I need to scroll down the page to find it.

[Memory]
Target is to maximize brightness. Currently in Settings app, looking for the "Display" menu item which requires scrolling.

[Observation]
The screen displays the Settings menu. The "Display" option is now clearly visible in the list, with the subtitle "Dark theme, font size, brightness". Other visible options include Sound & vibration, Wallpaper & style, Accessibility, Security, Privacy, Location, and Safety & emergency.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings ✓ | Step 3: Adjust the brightness slider to maximum →

[Decision]
Based on the current plan to access the Display settings, and observing that the "Display" option is now on the screen, I need to tap on it to enter the specific menu where the brightness controls are located.

[Memory]
Target is to maximize brightness. Successfully located the "Display" menu item; next step is to find the brightness slider inside it.

[Observation]
The screen displays the "Display" settings page. Under the "Brightness level" section, the text indicates "100%". Other visible settings include "Lock screen", "Screen timeout", "Dark theme" (currently off), and "Screen saver".

[Reflection]
The previous plan anticipated needing to adjust a brightness slider (Step 3). However, the observation shows that the "Brightness level" is already at 100%, meaning the target state has already been met without needing further adjustment.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Display settings ✓ | Step 3: Verify brightness level is at maximum ✓ | Step 4: Task complete →

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the "Brightness level" is already set to 100%. Since the user's instruction is to "Turn brightness to the max value", and it is already at the maximum possible value (100%), no further actions are required. I can declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Target is to maximize brightness. Verified in Display settings that "Brightness level" is already at 100%. Task objective achieved.
